sections.paragraph:
ἀποπλέω 1 aor. ἀπέπλευσα (s. πλέω; Hom. et al.; PLille 3, 5 [III b.c.]; Jos., Ant. 16, 16 al.) nautical t.t. sail away ἐκεῖθεν Ac 20:15. W. εἰς to indicate destination (Thu. 6, 61, 6; PEdgar 6, 3=Sb 6712 [258/7 b.c.]) 13:4; 14:26; 27:1.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PLille PLille = Papyrus grecs de Lille—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 119
